query: Argentina April 2026 inflation IPC rate INDEC estimates private consultants
answer:
Excellent — I obtained very comprehensive data. Let me compile the final research report.

---

## Argentina April 2026 Inflation (IPC): INDEC Data & Private Consultant Estimates

### Official INDEC Status

**The April 2026 official INDEC IPC figure has NOT yet been published** as of May 4, 2026. INDEC typically releases the previous month's CPI around the 12th–15th of the following month, so the April figure is expected around **May 12–15, 2026**.

### Recent Official INDEC Monthly IPC Trajectory

The most recent official data shows a concerning re-acceleration after inflation hit a low of ~1.5% in mid-2025:

| Month | Monthly IPC Rate | Index (Base Dec-2016) |
|---|---|---|
| May 2025 | 1.50% | 8,714.49 |
| June 2025 | 1.62% | 8,855.57 |
| July 2025 | 1.90% | 9,023.97 |
| August 2025 | 1.88% | 9,193.24 |
| September 2025 | 2.08% | 9,384.09 |
| October 2025 | 2.34% | 9,603.86 |
| November 2025 | 2.47% | 9,841.36 |
| December 2025 | 2.85% | 10,121.37 |
| January 2026 | 2.88% | 10,413.03 |
| February 2026 | 2.90% | 10,714.63 |
| **March 2026** | **3.38%** | **11,077.06** |

**Source:** INDEC via datos.gob.ar API

### Private Consultant Estimates for April 2026

Per an **Infobae article published May 4, 2026**, all major consultoras agree that April showed **deceleration** from March's 3.38%:

| Consultora | April 2026 Estimate | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **C&T** | **2.4%** | Measured in Gran Buenos Aires; ~1 p.p. below March |
| **Eco Go** | **2.5%** | Based on weekly food price tracking |
| **LCG** | ~1.7% (food only) | Food & beverages; full IPC consistent with ~2.4–2.5% |

**Key sectoral drivers:**
- **Food & beverages**: Sharp deceleration — C&T measured ~1% in April (down from 3%+ in prior months); meat up only 2%; fruits/vegetables declined
- **Education**: Seasonal normalization — fell from 12% in March to ~5% in April
- **Transport**: Rose ~4%, driven by fuel prices (oil prices + March carryover)
- **Clothing**: Above-average due to autumn-winter changeover
- **Housing/utilities**: Lower pressure from gas and electricity

### BCRA REM Survey (March 2026 Edition)

The BCRA's Relevamiento de Expectativas de Mercado (REM), surveyed March 27–31 with **46 participants**, projected:

| Metric | April 2026 IPC Forecast |
|---|---|
| **Median** | **2.6%** |
| **Mean** | **2.6%** |
| **Top 10 forecasters** | **2.7%** |
| Maximum | 3.0% |
| Minimum | 1.3% |
| Core IPC (median) | 2.4% |

**Important caveat:** The REM was surveyed *before* March's actual 3.38% was known (participants had estimated March at 3.0%), so these forecasts may have been slightly optimistic.

### Consensus Summary

| Source | April 2026 IPC Estimate |
|---|---|
| REM median (late March survey) | 2.6% |
| REM Top 10 (late March survey) | 2.7% |
| C&T (real-time measurement) | 2.4% |
| Eco Go (projection) | 2.5% |

**Consensus range: 2.4%–2.7%**, with real-time consultora measurements clustering at **2.4–2.5%**. This represents meaningful deceleration from March's 3.38%.

**Interannual rate**: Expected to drop from 32.6% to approximately **32.1%** (C&T estimate).

### Forward-Looking Pressures (May 2026)

Consultoras flagged building inflationary pressure for May:
- Middle East conflict driving up LNG and diesel import costs (~30%)
- Bus fares in CABA/province rose 5.4% from May 1
- Subway fares increased (1,414 → 1,490 pesos)
- New Metrogas residential tariffs effective May
- Highway toll and fuel tax increases

The REM median forecast for May 2026 was 2.3%, but this was made before these pressures materialized.

---

key_facts: Argentina's April 2026 official INDEC IPC has NOT yet been published as of May 4, 2026; expected release around May 12-15, 2026
March 2026 official INDEC IPC was 3.38% monthly, a significant jump from February's 2.90%, and the highest since early 2025
All major private consultoras agree April 2026 showed deceleration from March, with estimates clustering at 2.4%-2.5%
Consultora C&T estimated April 2026 IPC at 2.4% (measured in Gran Buenos Aires), approximately 1 p.p. below March
Consultora Eco Go projected April 2026 IPC at 2.5% based on weekly food price tracking
BCRA REM survey (46 participants, surveyed March 27-31) median forecast for April 2026 was 2.6%, mean 2.6%, Top 10 forecasters 2.7%
REM survey was conducted before March's actual 3.38% was known (participants estimated March at 3.0%), suggesting April forecasts may be slightly optimistic
REM core IPC (IPC Nucleo) median forecast for April 2026 was 2.4%
Food & beverages decelerated sharply in April — C&T measured ~1% (down from 3%+ in prior months)
Education seasonal spike ended: 12% in March dropped to ~5% in April
Interannual inflation expected to drop from 32.6% to approximately 32.1% if April comes in around 2.4-2.5%
Inflation has been re-accelerating since May 2025 low of 1.50%, climbing to 3.38% in March 2026 before the April deceleration
Significant inflationary pressures building for May 2026: Middle East conflict driving up energy import costs ~30%, transport fare increases, fuel tax hikes
REM median annual inflation forecast for 2026 (Dec-on-Dec): 29.1%; for next 12 months interannual: 23.8%
